title: Support option to rename `TOP` in --main generated file
body:
Hello,

I'm moving away from xsim to verilator and have a big library of GTKWave `.gtkw` files for viewing traces of all my simulations. When I generate trace files both programs emit different scopes, rendering my `.gtkw` files unusable for verilator. Is there any way to fix this?

This is an example Verilog file:

```verilog
initial begin
   $dumpfile("AXILiteInterface.vcd");
   $dumpvars(0, Test_AXILiteInterface);
end
```

I run verilator with:

```bash
verilator --timing --top Test_AXILiteInterface --trace -- binary ...
```

The scope views for xsim and verilator, respectively, are:

![image](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/1532903/60544bb9-01ab-48b5-9e3c-b8de73e4073e)
![image](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/1532903/155fc2f2-538e-456a-af7a-067321a21d87)

I can obviously post-process the output as part of my build process but it would be nice if I can get verilator to behave the same as xsim.

Thanks,
\- Don
